ranking.reason: Highest alert count (2 critical alerts), earliest availability drop among data-plane services
2 critical alerts, availability dropped 2s after index
1 critical alert, error_rate 100% — but downstream symptom, not root cause
1 warning alert, availability 0% but lower severity classification

gaps_identified.gap: RCA cannot identify operator action as root cause
gaps_identified.detail: Pipeline detects service-level anomalies but has no signal for operator commands. The actual root cause (over-broad operator command) is invisible to metric-based detection. RCA picks index-1 as root because it has the most critical alerts, but index-1 is a VICTIM, not the cause.

Pipeline sees 5 services drop simultaneously but cannot distinguish between 'operator stopped all' vs 'cascading failure from one service'. Simultaneous-drop pattern should trigger a different analysis path (operator action / infrastructure event) rather than service-level RCA.
